title: keteleeria fortunei subsp. oblonga
intro: Keteleeria fortunei subsp. oblonga, also known as Chinese fir, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of China. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, and it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Keteleeria fortunei subsp. oblonga include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are commonly used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
